implementation_summary: A 2D Canvas portrait auto-battler where the player drags weapon turrets from a bottom UI roster onto designated slots on their castle. Placed weapons automatically fire ballistic projectiles at the enemy castle. The game ends when either castle's health reaches zero.

gameplay.objective: Destroy the enemy castle before the player's castle is destroyed.
gameplay.primary_interaction: Drag and drop weapon icons from the bottom UI roster into empty slots on the player's castle.
gameplay.loss_or_timeout_condition: Player castle health reaches 0 or 30 seconds elapse.

entities.behavior: Static image drawn at the back z-index, scaled to cover the canvas.
Static structure on the left side. Has a health bar above it. Contains 3 invisible drop-target slots for weapons.
Static structure on the right side. Has a health bar above it. Pre-equipped with enemy weapons that auto-fire.
Spawns in the bottom UI roster. Can be dragged. If released over a valid player_castle slot, it snaps into place and begins auto-firing player_projectiles on a timer.
Pre-placed on the enemy castle. Auto-fires enemy_projectiles at the player castle on a timer.

Spawns from placed player_weapons. Follows a ballistic arc (velocity + gravity) towards the enemy castle. On collision with enemy_castle AABB, deals damage, triggers explosion particles, and despawns.
Spawns from enemy_weapons. Follows a ballistic arc towards the player castle. On collision, deals damage, triggers explosion particles, and despawns.
A semi-transparent rectangle at the bottom of the screen holding draggable player_weapons.
Two filled rectangles (Blue for player, Red for enemy) drawn above the respective castles, width scaling with current health percentage.

asset_plan.processing: Base64 encode into HTML audio tag
Base64 encode into HTML audio tag

interaction_flow.system_response: Enemy castle begins firing projectiles slowly. UI roster populates with Weapon_1 icons.

interaction_flow.player_action: Touch and drag Weapon_1 from the UI roster
interaction_flow.system_response: Weapon sprite follows the pointer. Valid drop slots on the Blue Castle are highlighted.

interaction_flow.player_action: Release Weapon_1 over a valid slot on Blue Castle
interaction_flow.system_response: Weapon snaps to the slot, plays Sfx.wav, and immediately begins auto-firing Projectile_1 at the Red Castle.
Projectiles follow ballistic arcs. Upon hitting castles, health bars decrease, floating damage numbers appear, and Sfx.wav plays.
Losing castle triggers a particle explosion. End card overlay appears with a 'Play Now' CTA.

parameters.name: player_castle_health
parameters.gameplay_effect: Determines how long the player can survive enemy fire.

parameters.name: enemy_castle_health
parameters.gameplay_effect: Determines how many player projectiles are needed to win.

parameters.name: player_fire_rate_ms
parameters.gameplay_effect: How often placed player weapons fire. Lower is faster.

parameters.name: enemy_fire_rate_ms
parameters.gameplay_effect: How often the enemy castle fires. Controls difficulty pressure.

parameters.name: projectile_gravity
parameters.gameplay_effect: Affects the arc and travel time of projectiles.

acceptance_criteria: Game renders entirely in a single HTML file using Canvas 2D with no external dependencies.
Player can drag Weapon_1 from the bottom roster and drop it onto Blue Castle.
Placed weapons automatically fire Projectile_1 in a ballistic arc towards Red Castle.
Enemy castle automatically fires Projectile_2 in a ballistic arc towards Blue Castle.
Projectiles colliding with castles reduce the respective health bar and trigger a simple particle explosion.
Game ends and displays a CTA overlay when either health bar reaches 0.
Audio plays on weapon placement and projectile impact.
non_goals_v1: Complex physics-based destruction of castle chunks (using simple health bars and particle bursts instead).
Unit mortality (placed weapons cannot be destroyed individually in V1).
Using .psb character assets (using Weapon_1 and Weapon_2 as the turrets to avoid unsupported formats).
